English_Original:
The frontier is the void between earth and the virtual universe.
<cb>1/ How a LW can end up in the frontier</c>
- devirtualized while all scanners are occupied
- devirtualized with DNA alteration or absence of DNA sequences
- devirtualized from a sector without having its devirtualization codes or the Skidbladnir close enough
- virtualized in a sector that doesn’t exist anymore (in most situations, if the target isn’t there anymore, the LW will end up somewhere else on that sector)
- RTTP (return to the past) while being under the scyphozoa’s grasp

Caution : 
- if the virtual world in which the LW is located is destroyed, the LW is lost

<cb>2/ Get a LW out of the frontier</c>
Send another LW to a way tower or to the interface in the case of Sector 5, and use the “retrieve data” action. Aelita will perform it much faster than other LW.

English_Original:
<cb>1/ The DNA sequences</c>
- are possessed by each LW
- can be seen in the link window (command: link)

<cb>2/ Missing DNA sequences</c>
- if the Scyphozoa steals them, they must be retrieved from the Sector 5 Interface
- they are displayed in red in the link window
- constraints identical to the missing MaterKey

<cb>3/ Alterations: DNA Fusion, DNA Inversion, DNA Confusion, DNA Severance</c>
- an alteration changes the DNA color
- if the LW is devirted, the LW is transfered in frontier
- DNA fusion increases over time, at 100%, the LW is lost
- DNA confusion limits actions available in battle
- DNA dissociation makes Aelita unable to enter XANA-activated towers
- DNA inversion hasn't specific effects

<cb>4/ The MaterKey</c>
- is possessed by each LW
- as long as an LW is bound to Lyoko (does not possess its MaterKey), it can only be devirtualized by a 'Code: Earth' CD.
- the LW must be into the upper part of a tower, and use the interface to load the materkey CD program
- having the MaterKey (substituted by a CD or posessed by the LW) doesn't ensure good devirtualization. As shown above, DNA musn't be altered or missing.

<cb>5/ Missing MaterKey</c>
- LW will stay on virtual world in case of RTTP.
- LW will be lost if defeated on virtual world.

<cb>6/ XANAfication</c>
- if XANA takes permanent control of a LW, it does it by corrupting the LW's MaterKey.
- a Code: Liberator CD must running while LW is devirted on a virtual world to free him from XANA and make it land in the scanners.

English_Original:
Virtual elements consist of things that aren't from earth. (unlike LWs)
Those can be virtual worlds, sectors, towers, vehicles, clones, Marabounta...
They need a certain amount of energy to be created (<cr>compiled</c> and <cr>executed</c>).

Compilation needs to be done each time you want to execute a build, because it's based on the current state of the virtual world. 
That's the only way data can be materialized without being rejected by the <cr>balance law of virtual worlds</c>.
When a virtual build is destroyed ('dematerialized'), allocated energy goes back to the supercomputer it belongs to.
Overvehicle recompilation time can be shortened via the 5th sector interface.

If a tower activated by XANA is destroyed, the minimal time before the next XANA attack is split in half.

Lyoko's Mountain, Desert, Forest and Ice sectors are referred to as 'surface sectors' and each contains 10 towers.
Towers 1 & 10 are called 'waytowers' and allow to travel to the nearest sector.
If the destination sector / waytower is destroyed, the waytower will redirect to the new closest sector in Lyoko.
Vietnamese_Current:
Virtual elements consist of things that aren't from earth. (unlike LWs)
Those can be virtual worlds, khu vucs, thaps, vehicles, clones, Marabounta...
They need a certain amount of nang luong to be created (<cr>compiled</c> and <cr>executed</c>).

Cómpilation needs to be done each time you want to execute a build, because it's based on the current state of the virtual world. 
That's the only way data can be da vat chat hoa without being rejected by the <cr>balance law of virtual worlds</c>.
When a virtual build is destroyed ('deda vat chat hoa'), allocated nang luong goes back to the sieu may tinh it belongs to.
Overvehicle recompilation time can be shortened via the 5th khu vuc interface.

If a thap activated by XANA is destroyed, the minimal time before the next XANA attack is split in half.

Lyoko's Mởuntain, Desert, Forest and Ice khu vucs are referred to as 'surface khu vucs' and each contains 10 thaps.
Tháps 1 & 10 are called 'waythaps' and allow to travel to the nearest khu vuc.
If the destination khu vuc / waythap is destroyed, the waythap will redirect to the new closest khu vuc in Lyoko.

English_Original:
The Skidbladnir (or 'Skid') allows you to get out of Lyoko, travel through the Digital Sea (network), and reach Replikas.
Contrary to other vehicles, when not being used, the skidbladnir stays in the Garage Skid.

<cb>1/</c>Shields protect the hull. (when it is in the digital sea)
<cb>2/</c>If the shields are down, it's the hull that will get damage.
<cb>3/</c>When docked, the skid is protected by docking field, which regenerates shield and hull
<cb>4/</c>Once shield is refilled, modules will start repairing
<cb>5/</c>Docking to a tower does automatically deactivates its inhibitors.

All of Skidbladnir modules can be individually enabled, disabled, and damaged. Some of them only function within the sea, other outside:
[LISTSKIDMODULES]

Navskids can only be undocked from the skid when it's battling in the digital sea.

<cb>Auto-Pilot</c>
Skidbladnir emergency auto-pilot can be enabled to move safely the Skid back to the Skid Garage from as far as a replika.
Moving slowly, not using hubs, it won't trigger any alert in the Digital Sea, and you can pass time while using this feature.

<cb>Shields, hull, and docking field</c>
- navskid shield regenerate when docked to Skid
- navskid hull regenerate when docked to docked Skid
- skid docking shield must be fully charged so that the hull, shield, and modules can repair themselves automatically when docked
- docking fields, generated by the towers or the garage skid, only refilll through combine transm procedure (megascan)

English_Original:
Replikas are virtual worlds created by XANA, they differ in several areas:
- only contain one sector
- only have two towers
- the world's virtual heart cannot be reached in the virtual space
It's possible to get the status of their virtual heart by finding their password, just like the one in Lyoko's 5th sector.

Finally, you can use translation to send your LWs as spectrums into the factory that houses the Replika supercomputer to destroy it.

- only have two thaps
- the world's virtual heart cannot be reached in the virtual space
It's possible to get the status of their virtual heart by finding their password, just like the one in Lyoko's 5th khu vuc.

Finally, you can use translation to send your LWs as spectrums into the nha may that houses the Replika sieu may tinh to destroy it.

English_Original:
IFSCL's design goal is to be as close as possible to the original series. Though, some freedom had to be taken in order to give more consistency, logic, or gameplay interest:
- the DNA transfer is renamed to DNA exchange and works both ways
- in the Superscan, a deactivated tower's color is blue rather than green
- in the vmap: deactivated tower color is blue rather than green or orange
- in the vmap: every LW under definitive xanafication will be purple (as seen in one episode, where William appear purple on the map)
- in the vmap: every LW and monster has its own dedicated icon
- in the vmap: every LW has round icons, triangles are reserved for vehicles
- in the vmap: all vehicles are blue & have their own mini cards
- in the vmap: the render is wireframe green (many variations appears in the episodes)
- commands related to the fifth sector are named ‘Carthage’, as it’s better known under this name, even if the name of the sector really is 'Sector Five' (you can use ‘sector5’ if you want to)

English_Original:
Allies must be virtualized on Lyoko so they can protect Aelita, but they can also stay or return to Earth to protect the player from the attacking creature.

<cb>CREATURE ASSAULT</c>
- The creature will repeatedly try to break into the lab in order to attack the player or allies
- Without any allies around (scanner room, lab, etc.), there won't be anybody to lure the creature out, or to wake the player after an assault; this leads to a game over
- By surviving an assault, the creature will reattack once the assault countdown ends
- Upon being hit, the player's vitality can decrease to a minimum of 1

<cb>TIPS</c>
- Keep at least one ally on Earth at all times to protect from assaults
- Unconscious allies cannot protect, they must be woken up!
- The creatures can be slowed down by locking the doors with elevator commands:
#elevator
#elevator.off

English_Original:
First the virtual map must be opened with:
#vmap
To locate friends, use:
#vmap.lwName
Once located, it automatically opens their 'comcard' (communication card).

Through the comcard, you indicate the LW where they should go (or follow).
The target can be a location (a tower for example), characters that are in the same sector, and more.

Once a target is reached, you might need to use the ACTION panel to perform additional maneuver (entering a tower, getting on a vehicle,...)

<cb>TIPS</c>
- Click on a LW's icon in the virtual map to open his comcard and lock the virtual map's camera on them
- Click on a LW's portrait in his comcard to lock the virtual map's camera on them

English_Original:
During a battle, if a navskid is critically damaged, it can end up in an adrift (purple color) state.
You have to finish the battle with the skid and then go over the navskid to retrieve it before it's out of bounds.
Note that sometimes multiple navskids will go in different directions, forcing you to choose. 

If a navskid goes beyond the boundaries, its signal is scrambled.
You should then use the CD and supersonar commands:

[supersonar]

Once you found the location of the signal (recon zone), approach the location through the skid, the closer you'll be from the navskid location, the smaller the zone will be.

English_Original:
Several steps are necessary to create a program.
It starts with a simple floppy disk and ends with a CD containing a program ready to install or execute.
Vietnamese_Current:
Several steps are necessary to create a chuong trinh.
It starts with a simple floppy disk and ends with a CD containing a chuong trinh ready to install or execute.

English_Original:
If there's no corresponding floppy disk in inventory, one must be taken from the drawers in Jeremy's bedroom.
The floppy-disk can then be inserted in the bedroom's computer or the laptop.
The new program can then be created.

<cb>TIPS</c>
The supercomputer does not have a floppy disk reader.
Vietnamese_Current:
If there's no corresponding floppy disk in inventory, one must be taken from the drawers in Jeremy's phong ngu.
The floppy-disk can then be inserted in the phong ngu's computer or the laptop.
The new chuong trinh can then be created.

<cb>TIPS</c>
The sieu may tinh does not have a floppy disk reader.
Floppy disk - Structure the chuong trinh

English_Original: Once floppy disk inserted, you must structure the future program with codeblocks. codeblocks names can be found inside the corresponding books: -The book related to the program that must be structured. -The programming book contains the names of all codeblocks. crossBookInfo Informations between the two books must be crossed to find the missing codeblocks names. 'Initialization' and 'Terminate' codeblocks are already there. codeBlocks There are several numbered slots into which the names of missing codeblocks can be entered. The final order of the codeblocks isn't important. #struct #struct.syncfrom.fd #struct.slotNumber.codeBlockName //ex: struct.2.detection_thread/$ #struct.slotNumber.reset //removes the codeblock name/$ #struct.simulate //when all the codeblocks are in place/$ #struct.save To send the data for revision to Aelita: #lyokoc #lyokoc.syncfrom.fd //NOT AVAILABLE ON SUPERCOMPUTER/$

'Initialization' and 'Terminate' codeblocks are already there. codeBlocks There are several numbered slots into which the names of missing codeblocks can be entered. The final order of the codeblocks isn't important. #struct #struct.syncfrom.fd #struct.slotNumber.codeBlockName //ex: struct.2.detection_thread/$ #struct.slotNumber.reset //removes the codeblock name/$ #struct.simulate //when all the codeblocks are in place/$ #struct.save To send the data for revision to Aelita: #lyokoc #lyokoc.syncfrom.fd //KHÔNGT KHA DỪNG ON SIEU MAY TINH/$

English_Original:
The macrocode editor can edit and fix the source code

First, a codeblock (in red) must be selected to then scroll its errors and correct them.
Once a line is selected, it can be rewritten following the programming rulebook notes.
Once all the lines of a codeblock are corrected, it must be then simulated to check for errors.

<cb>TIPS</c>
Macroeditor works best with keyboard control.
Focus on the window by double clicking it or using CTRL+TAB.
Arrow Keys = Navigate between lines.
Return Key = Select a block, a line...

The macrocode chinh suaor can chinh sua and fix the source code

First, a codeblock (in red) must be choned to then scroll its lois and correct them.
Once a line is choned, it can be rewritten following the chuong trinhming rulebook notes.
Once all the lines of a codeblock are corrected, it must be then simulated to check for lois.

<cb>TIPS</c>
Macrochinh suaor works best with keyboard control.
Focus on the window by double nhap vaoing it or using CTRL+TAB.
Arrow Keys = Navigate between lines.
Return Key = Chon a block, a line...

English_Original: In macrocode editor, a codeblock can be missing, as shown with a red 'data' warning. Use the locate (return key) to open the 'merge/import' sub window. mergedata If the codeblock file is found in your datas, it will be added to the list. TIPS Memorize the name of the codeblock missing from the list. Codeblocks files can be found and extracted from virtual packs.

TIPS Memorize the name of the codeblock missing from the list. Códeblocks files can be found and extracted from virtual packs.

English_Original:
Upon devirtualization, a LW must wait a certain number of Day Fragments (DF) before going back to Lyoko

Day Fragment = sunset, early morning, morning...

<color=yellow>RECOVERY</color>
Upon devirtualization, the LW falls into the RECOVERY state.
Virtualization is unavailable.
Once a mission ends, the LW falls into the TIRED state.

<color=orange>TIRED</color>
This state's duration depends on devirtualization:
-/ Devirt (manual or RTTP) =  4 DF.
-/ Devirt (by a monster) = 6 DF.
-/ Devirt/Revirt (by/through frontier) = 6 DF.
Virtualization available but very dangerous, on next devirt, the LW falls in EXHAUSTED state.

<color=red>EXHAUSTED</color>
This state duration depends on devirtualization:
-/ Devirt (manual or RTTP) =  12 DF.
-/ Devirt (by a monster) = 24 DF.
-/ Devirt/Revirt (by/through frontier) = 24 DF.
(strong chances not to be out of this state during next XANA attack)
Virtualization is unavailable.

<cb>TIPS</c>
If you want to send LW to Lyoko (mission creation) outside of a XANA attack, be careful not to run out of LW for when XANA will be able to attack!
For this, do not hesitate to consult the AGENDA.

English_Original:
The supercomputer, cooled by liquid nitrogen, continously draws energy from its nuclear battery.

This energy is needed continuously to keep virtual structures (like the world of Lyoko) and physical ones (scanners etc...) working. Energy is also drawn temporarily for the execution of programs like the Return to the Past.
With the energystat command, you can monitor the energy being used.

#energystat //displays energy usage/$

If you're running low on power, you can temporarily disable certain programs, such as scanners, using the dedicated commands for that program.
It's also possible to destroy sectors, but be careful, Lyoko's core defenses will decrease.

XANA, while inside the supercomputer, has access to its own supply of energy.
Vietnamese_Current:
The sieu may tinh, cooled by liquid nitrogen, continously draws nang luong from its nuclear battery.

This nang luong is needed continuously to keep virtual structures (like the world of Lyoko) and physical ones (may quets etc...) working. Nang luong is also drawn temporarily for the execution of chuong trinhs like the Return to the Past.
With the energystat command, you can monitor the nang luong being used.

#energystat //displays nang luong usage/$

If you're running low on power, you can temporarily disable certain chuong trinhs, such as may quets, using the dedicated commands for that chuong trinh.
It's also possible to destroy khu vucs, but be careful, Lyoko's core defenses will decrease.

XANA, while inside the sieu may tinh, has access to its own supply of nang luong.

Go down to the supercomputer room to arrange the circuits according to the notebook.

English_Original:
To use the programs, it is necessary to enter <cr>commands</c> in the commands console window:
#programName //starts or ends the program/$
#programName.command //classic command/$
#programName.<cb>parameter</c> //target command with changing value/$
#<cb>virtualObjectName</c>.programName //command to affect a pre-existing virtual object/$
#programName.<cb>subProgramName</c> //command to execute a sub-program/$

#programName.min //to minimize the window/$
#programName.max //to maximize the window/$

ctrl + TAB //to select the next window with keyboard inputs/$

<cb>TIPS</c>
There are two choices for command syntax that can be selected in the settings menu:

-/ The default "dot" syntax uses dots as separators between the parts of a command. The separator . can additionally be replaced by any of these: ,  ;  :  / . 
-/ -/ Example: virt.to.desert.1

-/ The "parenthesis" syntax, on the other hand, wraps commands and parameters between parenthesis
-/ -/ Example: virt(to)(desert)(1)

<cg>Some of the future commands are written in grey, they aren’t available yet.</c>
Vietnamese_Current:
To use the chuong trinhs, it is necessary to enter <cr>commands</c> in the commands console window:
#chuong trinhName //starts or ends the chuong trinh/$
#chuong trinhName.command //classic command/$
#chuong trinhName.<cb>parameter</c> //muc tieu command with changing value/$
#<cb>virtualObjectName</c>.chuong trinhName //command to affect a pre-existing virtual object/$
#chuong trinhName.<cb>subChương trinhName</c> //command to execute a sub-chuong trinh/$

#chuong trinhName.min //to minimize the window/$
#chuong trinhName.max //to maximize the window/$

ctrl + TAB //to chon the next window with keyboard inputs/$

<cb>TIPS</c>
There are two choices for command syntax that can be choned in the settings menu:

-/ The default "dot" syntax uses dots as separators between the parts of a command. The separator . can additionally be replaced by any of these: ,  ;  :  / . 
-/ -/ Example: virt.to.desert.1

-/ The "parenthesis" syntax, on the other hand, wraps commands and parameters between parenthesis
-/ -/ Example: virt(to)(desert)(1)

<cg>Some of the future commands are written in grey, they aren’t kha dung yet.</c>

English_Original:
<cb>Attacks</c> can happen (simultaneously) as soon as Xana has the initiative:
- activate a tower to damage things on Earth //ends when tower is deactivated/$
- attack the heart of Lyoko //ends when monsters are killed and no more with that order or when heart is destroyed/$
- attack the Skid in the Skid Garage //ends when monsters are killed and no more with that order or Skid destroyed/$
- use the Scyphozoa to temporarily steal energy through the Skid and destroy it at the same time //ends when the Scyphozoa is defeated or the Skid is destroyed/$

Once attack is ended, Xana's initiative is lost for some time (look out for his time marker in Agenda)

Unless you're on a Replika, Xana will not activate a new tower if you have activated one.

<cb>Reactions</c> results of LW presence, actions and/or xana attacks:
- using the Scyphozoa or the guardian on a LW
- destroy a sector of Lyoko with recently xanafied Aelita //only if Aelita is capable of doing so (an option that can be enabled at the start of the game)/$
- attack LW with monsters
- attack the Skid on a replika with monsters
- defend a replika by teleporting a LW definitely xanafied
- attack or defend a tower with monsters
- take control of a tower activated by Jeremy

- activate a thap to damage things on Earth //ends when thap is deactivated/$
- attack the heart of Lyoko //ends when quai vat are killed and no more with that order or when heart is destroyed/$
- attack the Skid in the Skid Garage //ends when quai vat are killed and no more with that order or Skid destroyed/$
- use the Scyphozoa to temporarily steal nang luong through the Skid and destroy it at the same time //ends when the Scyphozoa is defeated or the Skid is destroyed/$

Once attack is ended, Xana's initiative is lost for some time (look out for his time marker in Agenda)

Unless you're on a Replika, Xana will not activate a new thap if you have activated one.

<cb>Reactions</c> results of LW presence, actions and/or xana attacks:
- using the Scyphozoa or the guardian on a LW
- destroy a khu vuc of Lyoko with recently xanafied Aelita //only if Aelita is capable of doing so (an option that can be da kich hoat at the start of the game)/$
- attack LW with quai vat
- attack the Skid on a replika with quai vat
- defend a replika by teleporting a LW definitely xanafied
- attack or defend a thap with quai vat
- take control of a thap activated by Jeremy
